seoTitle: How to Import Data From Excel Into QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to import data from Excel into QuickBooks Online, from preparing your spreadsheet to mapping fields, so bulk entry stays fast and accurate.
intro: Entering large amounts of data by hand isn't practical when you already have it organized in Excel. This guide covers how to prepare your spreadsheet and import it into QuickBooks Online correctly.

steps.heading: Prepare your Excel file
steps.body: Organize your spreadsheet with clear column headers that match the type of data you're importing, such as invoices or customer lists.

steps.heading: Go to the import tool
steps.body: Navigate to the import data option within QuickBooks Online settings.

steps.heading: Upload your file
steps.body: Select and upload the prepared Excel file to begin the import process.

steps.heading: Map the fields
steps.body: Match each column in your spreadsheet to the corresponding field in QuickBooks Online before completing the import.

faq.question: What types of data can I import from Excel?
faq.answer: Common imports include customers, vendors, products, and invoices, depending on what QuickBooks Online supports for your plan.

faq.question: What happens if my column headers don't match exactly?
faq.answer: You can manually map mismatched columns to the correct fields during the import process rather than needing exact header names.

faq.question: Can I undo an import if something goes wrong?
faq.answer: Some imported data can be deleted in bulk afterward, but it's best to double-check your file before importing to avoid cleanup.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many rows I can import at once?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online typically has a row limit per import, so very large files may need to be split into batches.
